title.th: การบิณฑบาต

description.en: The daily alms round — the everyday meeting point between the saṅgha and lay supporters. These are among the first words a monk needs in either language.
description.th: การบิณฑบาตประจำวัน — จุดพบกันระหว่างพระสงฆ์กับญาติโยม เป็นคำพื้นฐานที่พระควรรู้

_review_note: DRAFT — Josh to verify Thai script, Paiboon+ romanization (should match the app's existing system), IPA, Pāli, and monastic register on every line.

vocab.note: The daily round to receive food offerings.
General respectful word for a monk.

vocab.english: to offer food into the bowl
vocab.note: The lay act of placing food in the bowl.
Used when giving to a monk or the Triple Gem.

vocab.english: to give thanks / rejoice in merit
vocab.note: The monk's blessing in return for an offering.

phrases.thai: นิมนต์ครับ
phrases.context: A layperson inviting a monk to receive / to come.

phrases.thai: ขอถวายอาหารครับ
phrases.english: I would like to offer food.
phrases.context: Spoken by a lay supporter at the alms round.

phrases.english: Blessings to you (monk speaking to laity)
phrases.context: A polite word a monk uses when addressing laypeople.

phrases.english: Sādhu — well said / so be it
phrases.context: Said to rejoice in and affirm merit.
